analysisSource: metaflow-cli-quality completion + mining data (mining-run.json, trail-sequences.json, discoverability-audit.json, synthesis-report.json)

opportunities.title: Undiscovered Advanced Flags: --assign, --next, --ready (swarm delegation)
opportunities.description: 13 documented flags are never used in real workflows. Of these, --assign, --next, --ready are critical for parallel execution (swarm dispatch protocol). Evidence: discoverability-audit.json shows 0% usage. trail-sequences.json shows orient commands dominate (189/251 unique commands). These flags enable DAG parallelization but are invisible in help text and examples.
evidence: discoverability-audit.json: 13 unused flags, --assign/--next/--ready explicitly listed
synthesis-report.json: workflow hints added but do not surface advanced flags
trail-sequences.json: no --assign or --next observed in 6 time windows across 251 commands
mining-run.json: 0 executions of flag-based features
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Add mandatory help examples showing --assign batch allocation (orient --assign --note '...'). 2) Add brief context in orient output when preGate nodes are available. 3) Document --next in 'Swarm worker protocol' help section with real example. 4) Test: verify --assign appears in top 3 help results for 'parallel' or 'batch'.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Unlock parallel execution for 3+ node DAGs. Expected: 30-40% faster batch completion on multi-node work.

opportunities.title: Hidden Advanced Flags: --json, --quiet (output control)
opportunities.description: --json (machine-readable output) and --quiet (suppress UI) flags exist but are never used. These enable scripting and automation. Evidence: 0 usage in mining-run.json. Likely blocked by lack of aliases (-j for --json, -q for --quiet) and missing examples showing scripting use cases.
evidence: discoverability-audit.json: --json, --quiet in unused list
synthesis-report.json: flag aliases added (-j, -q, -g, etc.) but adoption unknown
mining-run.json: no invocations with -j or -q flags
help system may not surface these as relevant to scripting workflows
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Add -j alias to --json in help (already coded, verify it's advertised). 2) Add -q alias to --quiet. 3) Update help text: 'Use -j for scripting/CI integration' as explicit example. 4) Test: grep for flag usage in a CI/scripting scenario (shell loop calling orient -j).
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Enable 100% of automation use cases. Unblock CI/tooling integrations that need machine-readable output.

opportunities.title: Workflow Abandon Rate: 78.8% (orient without reading → re-orient)
opportunities.description: Users run orient, then immediately re-orient without reading output (78.8% abandon rate = 149/189 orient calls). This suggests: (1) output is not immediately actionable, (2) context hints are not visible, or (3) users are uncertain what to do next. Evidence: trail-sequences.json shows repeated orient→orient patterns (e.g., 22 consecutive orients in 2026-03-01T22:40:00 window). Synthesis shows hints were added (orient→show, chart→show) but adoption is unclear.
evidence: discoverability-audit.json: workflowAbandonRate = 0.7884 (149 abandoned orients / 189 total)
trail-sequences.json: window 2026-03-01T22:40:00 contains 22 consecutive orient calls
synthesis-report.json: context-aware workflow hints added but no metric on hint effectiveness
mining-run.json: orient commands show contexts (nodeId, dagId) but no data on whether hints were read
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Add explicit 'Next Step' block to orient output showing recommended command (e.g., 'Next: roadmap show <nodeId>' or 'Next: roadmap chart'). 2) Measure hint effectiveness: instrument orient output to log when users follow suggested next step. 3) A/B test: try verbose hint (2 lines) vs minimal hint (1 line) and measure re-orient rate delta. 4) Add smart hint: if orient→orient pattern detected, offer 'Did you mean: roadmap show <nodeId>' as a redirect.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Reduce workflow abandon rate from 78.8% to <50% (target: 40%). Improve UX by making next action obvious. Expected: 30-40% fewer re-orients per session.

opportunities.title: Error Recovery Gap: 50% of errors not retried (2 failures in mining-run)
opportunities.description: Mining run shows 2 command failures (exitCode 1: claim and orient without args). Error recovery rate is 50% (1 error was NOT retried immediately). Evidence: mining-run.json shows 'claim init --owner test-agent' failed (exit 1), followed by 'orient' without args (also exit 1), then recovery with 'orient --note mine-6'. No retry between failures. Suggests error messages are not actionable or users don't know how to fix.
evidence: mining-run.json: claim command at index 11 exitCode=1, next orient at index 12 exitCode=1
discoverability-audit.json: errorWithoutRetryRate = 0.5 (50% of errors not retried)
no evidence of error message improvement or recovery suggestions in synthesis-report.json
discoverability score penalty: 10 points for errorWithoutRetryRate
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Enhance error messages for claim and orient to include recovery hint (e.g., 'claim failed: node not in current batch. Try: roadmap show <node>' or 'orient requires --note for state change. Try: roadmap orient --note <reason>'). 2) Add --help-on-error flag to repeat help for failing command. 3) Instrument error paths: log which error codes are not immediately retried, identify patterns. 4) Test: validate that 80%+ of first-time errors are retried within 2 command cycles.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Reduce error recovery time. Expected: 70%+ of errors retried on first attempt (up from 50%). Improve user confidence in CLI.

opportunities.title: Cache Retirement Flags Undiscovered: --cascade, --undo, --list
opportunities.description: Node retirement flags (--cascade, --undo, --list for 'roadmap retire') are not documented in main help. These are necessary for DAG corrections but require 'roadmap retire' discovery first. Evidence: discoverability-audit.json lists all three as unused. No retire examples in help or synthesis improvements.
evidence: discoverability-audit.json: --cascade, --undo, --list unused
synthesis-report.json: no mention of retire command improvements
help system likely only documents retire via subcommand, not flags
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Add 'roadmap retire' to main help with one-liner and flag summary. 2) Add example: 'roadmap retire node-id --cascade' with explanation. 3) Test: 'roadmap help retire' should list all three flags with brief descriptions.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Enable self-service DAG error recovery. Reduce need for manual intervention on failed nodes.

opportunities.title: Cross-Repo Visibility Flags: --repo, --global, --deps, --depth (trail + chart)
opportunities.description: Trail and chart cross-repo flags (--repo, --global, --deps, --depth) are unused. These enable visibility across dependent projects. Evidence: no usage in mining-run.json. Likely blocked by: (1) not surfaced in help, (2) users unaware of cross-repo patterns, (3) examples missing.
evidence: discoverability-audit.json: --repo, --global, --deps, --depth unused
mining-run.json: trail and chart used but no --repo or --global flags
synthesis-report.json: no improvements to trail/chart discoverability
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Add mandatory example to 'roadmap chart --help': 'See all dependencies: roadmap chart --deps' with sample output. 2) Add 'roadmap trail --global' to help with note about cross-project visibility. 3) For --repo and --depth: add examples with filter logic. 4) Test: verify help examples are discoverable and executable.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Unlock cross-project debugging and visibility. Enable users to understand DAG dependencies across repos.

opportunities.title: Validate Command Latency: 749ms avg (5.3× slower than orient)
opportunities.description: Validate command shows 749ms avg latency (mining-run.json index 6, index 17), compared to orient at 141ms avg. This is 5.3× slower. Likely causes: (1) validator recursion (checking all nodes), (2) file I/O for artifact verification, (3) shell invocation overhead. Blocking issue for pre-commit workflows that validate before complete.
evidence: mining-run.json: validate --note mine-validate = 749ms, validate --note mine-validate-2 = 731ms
orient: P50 = 141ms, P95 = 154ms (much faster)
synthesis-report.json: no validation latency improvements mentioned
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Profile validate command: add instrumentation to identify slowest validator (artifact-exists vs shell vs function). 2) Optimize high-frequency path: cache validator results for 30s if DAG unchanged. 3) Add --fast flag to skip shell validators (lint/test) and only check artifact-exists. 4) Parallel validators: run independent shell checks in parallel. Expected: reduce validate P50 from 749ms to <250ms.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Improve developer workflow velocity. Validate can run in pre-commit without friction. Expected: 3× faster validation.

opportunities.title: Command Error Classification: Claim and Orient Failures (2 errors)
opportunities.description: Two failures in mining run: (1) claim init --owner test-agent = exit 1 (reason unclear), (2) orient without args = exit 1 (expected). Mining-run.json does not capture error messages (only exit codes). Unable to diagnose root cause. Blocks understanding of failure patterns.
evidence: mining-run.json: claim cmd (index 11) exitCode=1, no stderrLines or error message captured
mining-run.json: orient cmd (index 12) exitCode=1, no error details
instrumentation added context fields (nodeId, dagId, noteText, stderrLines) but stderrLines may be empty or not collected
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Verify stderrLines is populated in all error cases. 2) Update mining script to capture and log error messages. 3) Classify errors: (a) permission (DAG not in state for operation), (b) invalid args (missing required flag), (c) logic (node already claimed), (d) system (file I/O). 4) Add test: verify each error class has actionable message.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Enable error root-cause analysis. Improve error recovery by categorizing failures. Block unknown failures from reaching users.

opportunities.title: Workflow Pattern Mining: Identify Successful vs Failing Sequences
opportunities.description: Trail-sequences.json shows command patterns over time but lacks analysis: Which sequences lead to completion? Which lead to abandonment? Pattern: 22× consecutive orient in window 2026-03-01T22:40:00 — likely failed attempts or confusion. No data on whether these were resolved or abandoned. To improve UX, need to: (1) detect failure patterns, (2) identify successful workflows, (3) route users to successful sequences.
evidence: trail-sequences.json: window 2026-03-01T22:40:00 has 22 consecutive orients (suspicious pattern)
synthesis-report.json: 'no clear winning pattern detected' — mining did not analyze success/failure correlation
discoverability-audit.json: no workflow success rate metric
mining-run.json: only 30 commands, insufficient for pattern analysis
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Extend mining: run 100+ command sequences sampled from real usage (cross-repo trail). 2) Classify sequences: (a) successful (orient→show→complete), (b) abandoned (orient→orient→...), (c) error-recovery (orient→error→orient→....). 3) Analyze: which flag combinations appear in successful sequences? 4) Output: pattern-success-rate.json with (pattern, successRate, avgCycles, topFlags). 5) Use findings to prioritize help examples (show successful patterns first).
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Reduce workflow abandonment by 20-30%. Identify and amplify successful user behaviors. Expected: empirical basis for help improvements.

opportunities.title: Discoverability Score Gap: 49/100 (target was 50)
opportunities.description: Final discoverability score is 49/100, just below the 50-point threshold (synthesis-report.json target: '≥50'). Calculated as: 100 - (0.0667×30) - (0.5×20) - (0.7884×30) - (0.7647×20) = 49. The largest penalty is workflowAbandonRate (23.65 points) and unusedFlagPenalty (15.29 points). This is not a test failure (test-success = 100%) but shows room for improvement in discoverability specifically.
evidence: discoverability-score.json: score = 49, just below target ≥50
synthesis-report.json: allTargetsMet = true (tests passed, but discoverability acknowledged as gap)
Components: workflowAbandonRate penalty = 23.65, unusedFlagPenalty = 15.29 dominate
Pass criteria listed discoverability as pass=false but allTargetsMet=true (score acknowledged as edge case)
opportunities.recommendation: 1) View as validation success but quality opportunity. 2) Implement opp-001, opp-002, opp-003 to raise score naturally (estimated: +10-15 points). 3) Do NOT add artificial discoverability metrics — focus on real UX. 4) Retarget: next phase should aim for 65+/100 by addressing workflow abandon and flag adoption.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Improve CLI discoverability from 49 to 65+ by addressing root causes (workflow hints, flag visibility, error recovery). Secondary benefit: demonstrate that discoverability improvements correlate with reduced abandonment.

opportunities.title: Cache Performance Validation: Token cache 396→1 scans, real-world measurement pending
opportunities.description: Token cache was added to eliminate 396-file scans on repeated listTokens() (synthesis-report.json: 'eliminated 396-file scans'). Lab measurement shows benefit but real-world impact unknown. Mining-run did not measure cache hit rates or I/O reduction. No evidence that cache is actually being used or that the 396-scan claim was verified in production.
evidence: synthesis-report.json: 'Token cache: eliminated 396-file scans on repeated listTokens()'
mining-run.json: no cache hit rate metrics or file scan counts
dogfood-metrics.json: no cache performance data
Likely: cache was implemented but adoption/effectiveness not measured
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Instrument cache: add metrics (hit rate, miss rate, files scanned with/without cache). 2) Run comparison: (a) warm cache case (repeated listTokens), (b) cold cache case (first call). 3) Measure: file I/O syscalls before and after. 4) Publish: include cache metrics in next mining-run. Expected: show 95%+ cache hit rate on repeated commands.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Verify cache is operational and delivering expected 396→1 reduction. Unblock future token-based optimizations (e.g., distributed caching).

opportunities.title: Integration Test Expansion: Untested Edge Cases (49 tests all passing)
opportunities.description: Integration test report shows 49 tests all passing. However, 100% pass rate on a small suite may hide edge cases. Observed failures in mining (claim, orient without args) suggest edge cases not covered. Test suite organized by (tests/cli/integration.test.ts: 31, tests/claims/claims-cache.test.ts: 8, tests/metaflow/command-instrumentation.test.ts: 10) but lacks signal on coverage of error paths.
evidence: integration-test-report.json: 49 tests, 0 failures, 100% pass rate
mining-run.json: 2 command failures (claim, orient) — suggest untested scenarios
No evidence of error-path or negative-case tests in test groups
Test organization is functional (cli, claims, metaflow) not coverage-based
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Add negative-test suite: test claim on wrong node state, orient without --note, invalid DAG references. 2) Measure: code coverage report for error handlers. 3) Add invariant tests: verify state consistency after error (partial writes not left behind). 4) Expand test suite: target 65+ tests covering error paths. Expected: catch 80% of edge cases before mining.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Reduce field failures. Improve error messages by testing them. Expected: catch 3-5 edge cases per quarter.

opportunities.title: Instrumentation Overhead: Context fields (nodeId, dagId, stderrLines) impact on latency
opportunities.description: Enhanced instrumentation added context fields (nodeId, dagId, noteText, stderrLines) to all command executions (synthesis-report.json). These are captured in mining-run.json contexts. Overhead not measured. If significant, could explain orient P50 of 141ms (vs expected <100ms for stateless operation).
evidence: synthesis-report.json: 'Enhanced command instrumentation with context fields'
mining-run.json: all commands include context blocks but no latency breakdown
Latency: orient P50 = 141ms (reasonable but not optimal for stateless operation)
No profiling data showing context collection cost
opportunities.recommendation: 1) Benchmark: measure orient latency with and without context instrumentation. 2) Profile: identify if stderrLines capture is blocking. 3) Optimize: consider async context collection if latency impact > 5%. Expected: context overhead < 10ms.
opportunities.estimatedBenefit: Low immediate benefit but good practice for future instrumentation decisions. Validate that monitoring cost is acceptable.

q1_workflow_hints_effectiveness.question: Did the workflow hints reduce abandonment rate?
q1_workflow_hints_effectiveness.answer: Unclear. Hints were added (orient→show, chart→show) but abandonment rate is still 78.8%. Either hints are not visible in output, not actionable enough, or users don't read them. Evidence: discoverability-audit.json shows unchanged 78.8% abandon rate post-implementation. Recommendation: implement opp-003 to measure and improve hint visibility.

q2_feature_adoption.question: Which new features are actually being used?
q2_feature_adoption.answer: Limited adoption observed: (1) Basic commands used frequently (orient 19%, chart 23%, show 10%, trail 10%, validate 7%). (2) Advanced features largely unused: --assign, --next, --ready (0% usage), --json, --quiet (0% usage), retire flags (0% usage). (3) Cache feature (token cache) implemented but effectiveness not measured in mining. Overall: core features adopted, advanced/parallel features invisible.

q3_top_pain_points.question: What are the top 3 user pain points remaining?
answer: Pain Point 1: Workflow uncertainty (78.8% abandon rate). Users run orient then re-orient without reading output, suggesting unclear next step. Root cause: output not actionable or hints too subtle.

Pain Point 2: Undiscovered advanced features. 13 flags remain unused (--assign, --next, --ready, --json, --quiet, etc.), blocking parallelization and scripting. Root cause: not surfaced in help, no examples, no aliases.
Pain Point 3: Error recovery friction. 50% of errors not immediately retried. Root cause: error messages not actionable (no recovery hints), users don't know how to fix.

q4_command_sequence_patterns.question: Are there patterns in command sequences that fail vs succeed?
q4_command_sequence_patterns.answer: Partial answer from trail-sequences.json: Observed patterns: (1) Success pattern likely: orient→show→<work>→complete. (2) Failure/confusion pattern: orient→orient→orient→orient (22× in one window). (3) Validation pattern: orient→validate→<fix>→complete. However, trail-sequences.json does NOT classify by success/failure outcome. Mining run did not include completion status, so correlation unknown. Recommend opp-009 to analyze real sequences with outcome labels.

q5_untapped_improvement.question: Where is the most untapped improvement potential?
answer: Highest leverage: Workflow hints and error recovery (opp-003, opp-004). Combined estimated benefit: reduce abandon rate from 78.8% to <50%, reduce error recovery cycles by 2×. Effort: medium, impact: very high.
Second highest: Flag discoverability (opp-001, opp-002, opp-005, opp-006). Combined: unlock parallelization and scripting. Effort: small-medium, impact: high for advanced users.
Third: Validate command optimization (opp-007). Latency 5.3× baseline suggests easy wins (caching, parallelization). Effort: medium, impact: medium (dev experience).

dataQualityNotes: Mining run (30 commands) is small sample; patterns should be validated with 100+ commands
Trail sequences show command types but lack outcome labels (completed vs abandoned)
Error root causes not captured (only exit codes, stderrLines may be empty)
Cache performance claimed but not measured (no hit rate data)
Help effectiveness unknown (hints added but no metric on whether users read them)

immediate: opp-003: Add explicit 'Next Step' block to orient output (1-2 line change, high impact)
opp-004: Enhance error messages with recovery hints for claim/orient failures
opp-001: Add help examples for --assign, --next, --ready with swarm use case
phase2: opp-009: Run expanded mining (100+ sequences) with outcome labels (success vs abandoned)
opp-007: Profile and optimize validate command (target: <250ms)
opp-002: Verify flag aliases (-j, -q) are advertised and working
phase3: opp-006: Document cross-repo flags (--repo, --global, --deps) with examples
opp-012: Expand integration tests to negative-path (error handling)
opp-011: Instrument and measure cache hit rates, validate 396→1 claim
